title: BIRTH CERTIFICATES - 211info
text: OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION BIRTH CERTIFICATES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF As of July 1, 2018, individuals who are homeless can visit a local homeless service provider or organization to get help ordering their Oregon birth certificate. Participating agencies can provide help with completing the birth record order form, provide information on documentation needed, provide a check for the payment, and prepare the affidavit for “Homeless Status for Fee-Exempt Certified Copy of Birth Certificate” to include with your order. 800 NE Oregon Street Portland, OR 97232 Eligibility: Individuals experiencing homelessness who were born in Oregon Hours: Not applicable Email (971) 673-1222 Get Directions Visit Website Phone/FAX Numbers (971) 673-1222 Main phone (971) 673-1299 FAX 711 TTY/TTD (800) 422-6012 Toll-free Email Address: [email protected] INTAKE PROCEDURE: Visit website for a list of participating agencies FEES: None DOCUMENTS: State ID card or Drivers license LANGUAGES: English, interpretation or signing by arrangement AREA SERVED: Clackamas, Columbia, Coos, Crook, Deschutes, Harney, Jackson, Lane, Linn, Malheur, Marion, Polk, Tillamook, Umatilla, Washington, and Yamhill Counties Services: Birth Certificates * Homelessness Issues Other Services Offered At This Location: COMMUNITY GROUPS AND GOVERNMENT/ADMINISTRATIVE OFFICES - OREGON MEDICAL MARIJUANA PROGRAM SPECIALIZED TREATMENT AND PREVENTION - VACCINES FOR CHILDREN SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - OREGON HOPELINE SUBSTANCE USE DISORDER SERVICES - TOBACCO QUIT LINE If you represent this agency, update your listing here . * The community resource directory information is up to date to the best of our knowledge. However, you should always call the provider to confirm this information and make an appointment. Be sure to confirm payment information with the provider, if payment is required. 211Info does not rate, recommend or endorse any agency. We simply provide information as a public service. OREGON HEALTH AUTHORITY PUBLIC HEALTH DIVISION
